summary: In this abstract, Walter C. Willett reviews the epidemiological and metabolic evidence supporting the Mediterranean diet. He emphasizes that replacing carbohydrates and trans fats with unsaturated fats, combined with a diet rich in whole foods and a healthy lifestyle, can prevent the vast majority of coronary heart disease, stroke, and type 2 diabetes.

principles.statement: The vast majority of cardiovascular and metabolic diseases are preventable through diet and lifestyle.
principles.rationale: Analyses show that combining regular physical activity, not smoking, and healthy food choices consistent with the Mediterranean diet can prevent the majority of heart disease, stroke, and diabetes.
principles.source_quote: ...over 80% of coronary heart disease, 70% of stroke, and 90% of type 2 diabetes can be avoided by healthy food choices that are consistent with the traditional Mediterranean diet.

principles.statement: Unsaturated fats are metabolically superior to carbohydrates for heart health.
principles.rationale: Controlled feeding studies demonstrate that monounsaturated and polyunsaturated fats improve lipid profiles (reducing LDL and triglycerides, increasing HDL) when compared to carbohydrates, especially in the presence of insulin resistance.
principles.source_quote: ...compared to carbohydrate, both monounsaturated and polyunsaturated fats reduce LDL and triglycerides and increase HDL cholesterol.

heuristics: Adopt elements of the traditional Mediterranean diet—such as higher intakes of fruits, vegetables, whole grains, fish, unsaturated fats, and moderate daily alcohol—to greatly benefit health.

signature_quotes.text: ...over 80% of coronary heart disease, 70% of stroke, and 90% of type 2 diabetes can be avoided by healthy food choices that are consistent with the traditional Mediterranean diet.
signature_quotes.context: Summarizing the massive preventative potential of diet and lifestyle choices.

signature_quotes.text: ...compared to carbohydrate, both monounsaturated and polyunsaturated fats reduce LDL and triglycerides and increase HDL cholesterol.
signature_quotes.context: Explaining the metabolic benefits of unsaturated fats over carbohydrates.

anti_patterns.description: Consuming trans fats from partially hydrogenated vegetable oils.
anti_patterns.why_it_fails: It is strongly related to an increased risk of heart disease and is entirely absent from traditional, heart-healthy Mediterranean diets.
